singapore.summary: If congestion pricing saves time, improves buses and funds better infrastructure, support will follow, says NUS’ Timothy Wong.

singapore.summary: Ship chartering is not what most people picture when they think of maritime. For Vale Tan, that is exactly what drew her in – and keeps her going.

us.summary: With President Trump in attendance, an all-superstar lineup that included Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber and Burna Boy felt a little more political than it might have otherwise.

us.title: State Department issues warning to Americans living and traveling abroad
us.summary: On Saturday, the State Department issued a warning to Americans living and traveling abroad, urging them to remain cautious in the face of increased threats on U.S. diplomatic facilities. “Due to heightened tensions in the Middle East, the security environment remains complex with the potential for unforeseen escalation,” the State Department wrote in a statement…

us.title: Epstein survivor on Blanche meeting: ‘We were gaslit’
us.summary: A survivor of convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein on Saturday said she was “gaslit” during her meeting last week with acting Attorney General Todd Blanche. Jess Michaels told MS NOW’s Alex Witt about this recent meeting, along with other survivors, with Blanche. She said that Blanche repeatedly denied that anything in the Department of Justice’s…

us.title: High-profile Trump nominees pose test for outgoing Republicans
us.summary: Several high-profile Trump administration nominees face committee votes in the coming weeks that will show whether outgoing GOP senators are willing to buck the administration — or at least exert some leverage. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) director nominee Erica Schwartz and attorney general nominee Todd Blanche received a tepid reception at their…

us.title: Warner rails against Trump’s primetime speech: ‘This guy is not looking for a secure election’
us.summary: Sen. Mark Warner (D-Va.) on Sunday said President Trump’s primetime speech shows that he’s not serious about securing elections, pointing to the recent firings of the remaining Democratic members at an independent election administration agency earlier this month. “There was something called the Elections Assistance Commission. Nobody knows about it, but it works with local…

us.title: Energy secretary: US ‘will continue to assure flow of traffic’ through Strait of Hormuz amid reignited Iran war
us.summary: Energy Secretary Chris Wright on Sunday said the U.S. “will continue to assure flow of traffic” through the Strait of Hormuz more than a week after hostilities between the U.S. and Iran resumed. “The president is always looking for the diplomatic off-ramp,” Wright told ABC News’s Jonathan Karl in an interview on “This Week.” “President…

us.title: US service member killed in Iraq during ‘controlled detonation’ of Iran drone: Centcom
us.summary: A U.S. service member was killed in northern Iraq on Saturday during a “controlled detonation” of unexploded ordnance from a downed Iranian one-way attack drone, the U.S. Central Command (Centcom) said on Sunday. Another service member was injured and continues to receive medical treatment for a “minor” injury, according to Centcom. The announcement marks the…

us.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
us.summary: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.

asia.title: China roadshow to boost FDI inflow
asia.summary: A government investment roadshow in China could help attract businesses from Chongqing and Sichuan, two major manufacturing hubs for high-tech industries, says CP Group Senior Vice-Chairman Suphachai Chearavanont.

asia.title: US military reports another soldier killed in northern Iraq
asia.summary: The US military on Sunday reported the death of another US soldier, bringing the total US military death toll in the Iran war to 17. In a post on social media, US Central Command (CENTCOM) said a soldier was killed in northern Iraq on Saturday during a controlled detonation of unexploded ordnance from a downed Iranian one-way attack drone. A second soldier sustained minor injuries and is continuing to receive medical treatment, it said. This came a day after the US military reported that two...

asia.title: 2.76m seek welfare eligibility review
asia.summary: About 2.76 million people who failed to qualify for the 2026 state welfare card scheme have appealed for a review of their eligibility, says the Finance Ministry.

asia.title: Inside India’s cybercrime investigations: How scammers exploit Telegram
asia.summary: From exam-paper leaks to investment scams, Telegram has repeatedly featured in India’s cybercrime cases. CNA speaks to investigators about why scammers favour the platform - and what Asia can learn from India’s response.

asia.title: Hong Kong’s 5-year plan will shore up market with governance and vision
asia.summary: Hong Kong’s decision to introduce its first five-year plan has prompted familiar questions. Does this signal greater government intervention at the expense of the free market? Will it make Hong Kong’s development model more similar to that of the mainland? Could it overlap with the annual policy address and budget? And without detailed key performance indicators, might it become little more than a symbolic document? These concerns deserve a serious response. For Hong Kong, the purpose of a...

asia.title: BTS, Madonna and the Muppets light up Fifa World Cup half-time show
asia.summary: Musical headliners Madonna, Shakira, Justin Bieber, BTS and Coldplay were joined by Brazilian football icons and Ted Lasso actors in a spectacular first-ever Fifa World Cup final half-time show on Sunday. The star-packed, Super Bowl-style extravaganza – a first for football’s showpiece event – also featured a tribute to football icon Pele, and a rendition of “Seven Nation Army” led by Gustavo Dudamel and featuring musicians from the US, Venezuela and Iran. Sesame Street and The Muppets...

asia.title: Israelis march to Gaza border demanding re-establishment of settlements
asia.summary: Thousands of Israelis, including settlers and right-wing activists, marched towards the border with Gaza on Sunday, calling on the government to re-establish Israeli settlements in the war-shattered Palestinian territory, an Agence France-Presse journalist reported. The demonstrators gathered near the border before walking in large numbers towards the fence overlooking northern Gaza, where Israeli security forces deployed to prevent participants from advancing further. Agence France-Presse...

asia.title: Trump and Infantino set to face red-card protest at Fifa World Cup final
asia.summary: Football fans launched a unique protest against US President Donald Trump and Fifa chief Gianni Infantino at Sunday’s World Cup final. Red cards were handed out in New York and New Jersey to those on their way to the MetLife Stadium for the Spain versus Argentina clash. Protest organisers hope fans will hold up the red cards during the handing over of the trophy to demonstrate their anger at Trump and Infantino. The duo are set to give the winning captain the World Cup. The red cards featured a.

tech.summary: According to the New York Times, federal agents have been told that the FBI will no longer be investigating confrontations involving ICE agents. The DHS and DOJ denied the change in policy to The Times. The reported change in guidance follows renewed scrutiny of violence by ICE agents who have killed two people in the […]

tech.title: Orchid is a delightfully retro and approachable hipster synth
tech.summary: In 2017, I bought an old Magnus chord organ off Craigslist for $10. It's one of my favorite music gear purchases. Electric chord organs let you play full chords with just a press of a button, making them perfect for aspiring singer-songwriters or fooling around at home. But chord organs largely fell out of favor […]

briefing: Global tensions remain elevated, with the US State Department issuing a warning to Americans living and traveling abroad due to heightened threats on US diplomatic facilities, while the US military reported another soldier killed in northern Iraq, bringing the total US military death toll in the Iran war to 17. In Singapore, concerns have been raised over the Singapore Pools app being used to bet from overseas, despite rules against it, with users reportedly finding workarounds to place bets from countries such as Thailand, Malaysia, and China. The Straits Times Index remained stable, while the technology sector saw the release of the Kodak EC35, a dirt-cheap point-and-shoot film camera, and reports of scammers exploiting Telegram in India's cybercrime cases, highlighting the need for increased vigilance in the digital space.

digests.singapore: Transportation and infrastructure development are being explored in Singapore, with suggestions for congestion pricing in Johor Bahru and a successful pilot at Woodlands Hospital to reduce hospitalisations. Meanwhile, law enforcement is dealing with various scams and illegal activities, including phone scams involving numbers starting with the digit 3, fraudulent registration of SIM cards, and the sale of illegal tortoises on social media platforms. These issues highlight the need for continued vigilance and regulation in areas such as technology, wildlife trade, and financial transactions.
digests.us: The US government is navigating various international and domestic challenges, including heightened tensions in the Middle East, with the State Department warning Americans abroad to exercise caution and the Energy Secretary assuring the flow of traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, President Trump's administration is facing scrutiny over its nominees and actions, with Senator Mark Warner criticizing Trump's approach to election security and a survivor of Jeffrey Epstein's abuse expressing frustration with a meeting with acting Attorney General Todd Blanche. The US is also dealing with the aftermath of a fatal incident in Iraq involving a downed Iranian drone, which resulted in the death of a US service member.
digests.asia: Economic development and investment are key themes in the region, with a Chinese roadshow aiming to attract businesses from major manufacturing hubs, while Hong Kong's new five-year plan seeks to shore up the market with governance and vision. In contrast, geopolitical tensions and conflict are evident in reports of a US soldier killed in northern Iraq and Israeli marches to the Gaza border demanding the re-establishment of settlements. Meanwhile, social welfare and cybercrime are also significant concerns, with millions seeking welfare eligibility reviews in Thailand and scammers exploiting Telegram in India.
digests.others: Tensions are escalating in the Middle East, with Israeli ministers marching to demand the return of settlements near Gaza, while the US reports another soldier killed in an Iranian attack in Iraq, and Russia launches a major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities. Meanwhile, a ferry disaster off the coast of Guyana has resulted in dozens of people missing, with 67 rescued so far, including 15 children. Algeria is seeking to expand its energy role in the EU, particularly with Germany, amid regional tensions and instability in the Sahel and Western Sahara.
digests.tech: The intersection of technology and art is a recurring theme, with innovations such as unpronounceable glyph-based music projects and AI-generated songs pushing the boundaries of creativity. Meanwhile, advancements in fields like photography and music production are making high-quality equipment more accessible, as seen with the release of affordable film cameras like the Kodak EC35 and retro-style synthesizers like the Orchid. Additionally, the role of technology in monitoring and prevention is being explored in areas such as disease prevention and insurance-coverage decisions, with AI being piloted as a potential solution to streamline processes.
digests.economist: The Economist's analysis highlights the rising influence of China's new billionaires and India's resilience in the face of energy shocks, while also exploring the societal challenges faced by countries such as China, where gender antagonism is exacerbating demographic problems. The publication also examines the impact of bureaucracy on nightlife in America and the selective crackdown on Cambodia's scam industry, driven by sanctions and pressure from major powers. Additionally, the articles touch on the competitive dynamics in the tech industry, as seen in SK Hynix's dominance in advanced memory chips, and the high-stakes elections in Israel.
